benchmarks.name: Rust (native)
benchmarks.value: Full API (47 store op modules + TEP exchange layer)

benchmarks.name: Python
benchmarks.value: PyO3 driver with epistemic types — BoundedFloat, ConfidenceType, ViewState, WatMetrics, entity CRUD, type stubs (branch)
benchmarks.source: Branch session/conn-pillar. First-class Python classes: BoundedFloat, ConfidenceType, ViewState (three-state). WatMetrics with Prometheus text rendering. APIs: list_entities(), entity_count(), get_sections(), delete_section(). Type stubs (wikai_engine.pyi) for IDE autocompletion. Pending: Lens API exposure, TEP exchange types.

benchmarks.name: Swift (Apple native)
benchmarks.value: C-FFI wrapper + SwiftUI demo — iOS, watchOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS. 5 static lib targets verified, 4 FFI tests
benchmarks.source: PR #148 (merged). TrustDbNvm.swift: idiomatic wrapper with NvmCapabilities, NvmBatteryState, NvmBenchmarkResult, NvmRegion types. TrustDbNvm.h: C bridging header. #[no_mangle] extern 'C' FFI for lifecycle, capabilities, battery-aware persistence, byte-addressable I/O, messaging benchmark. Static libs for 5 targets: aarch64-apple-ios, aarch64-apple-ios-sim, aarch64-apple-watchos, aarch64-apple-watchos-sim, aarch64-apple-darwin. Plus: SwiftUI demo app (TrustDbDemoiOS) with auto-detected hardware display, battery-aware persistence tier picker, 100K ops iMessage benchmark. Xcode project via xcodegen. All hardware auto-detected via HardwareProfile::detect().

benchmarks.name: Apple Ecosystem Integration
benchmarks.value: Battery-aware sync, Watch/AirPods delta sync (BLE), Vision Pro spatial scoping (branch)
benchmarks.source: PR #218 branch. Power-shaped sync: Charging=aggressive, Balanced=normal, LowBattery=conservative, LPM=read-only (205 LOC). Delta sync: binary deltas sized for Watch BLE (~50KB/s) and AirPods (~20KB/s) (169 LOC). Spatial-temporal scoping: geofence, named zones, bounding boxes, Vision Pro room-aware sync, Haversine distance (158 LOC). This is the 'always-on intelligence' layer — TrustDB adapts to each Apple device's power, bandwidth, and spatial context automatically.

comparisons.source: No power-aware scheduling, no spatial scoping
Partition-based, no battery awareness, no spatial
No BLE delta optimization, no governance

sections.narrative: The drivers story has transformed from Rust-only to a three-language foundation. Swift is now a first-class citizen: C-FFI wrapper (TrustDbNvm.swift) with idiomatic types, static libraries verified for all 5 Apple targets (iOS, iOS Simulator, watchOS, watchOS Simulator, macOS), a SwiftUI demo app with hardware auto-detection and battery-aware persistence, and Xcode project generation. This directly competes with Core Data and Realm — TrustDB is the only embedded database with governance-aware sync across the entire Apple device fleet (Mac, iPhone, iPad, Watch, Vision Pro, AirPods). Python driver (branch) adds epistemic types as first-class objects: BoundedFloat, ConfidenceType, ViewState three-state, with type stubs for IDE autocompletion. Beyond basic drivers, the Apple ecosystem integration (PR #218) adds power-shaped sync (battery-aware scheduling), delta sync for BLE-constrained devices (Watch, AirPods), and spatial-temporal scoping (Vision Pro room-aware). No competitor offers this depth of Apple integration. Gap: JavaScript/Node.js driver and Lens API exposure in Python.

history.note: PR #148 (merged) — TrustDbNvm.swift, C bridging header, 5 Apple targets, SwiftUI demo, 4 FFI tests
Branch session/conn-pillar — BoundedFloat, ViewState, WatMetrics, entity CRUD, type stubs
PR #218 — power-shaped sync, Watch/AirPods delta sync, Vision Pro spatial scoping

benchmarks.name: Lens Core Types + Filters
benchmarks.value: Implemented (branch session/tep-g2-lens) — confidence, temporal, spatial, classification filters
benchmarks.source: WN-TEP-06: Lens core type with composable filters (confidence threshold, temporal range, spatial bounding, classification chain). WN-TEP-07: Lens composition algebra (intersect/union at plan level). WN-TEP-08: Lens compilation to TraversalPlan (cacheable, serializable). WN-TEP-09: Lens serialization as GovernanceArtifact (every query produces audit record). On branch session/tep-g2-lens, pending merge.

benchmarks.name: Wire Protocol (gRPC)
benchmarks.value: trustdb.proto + tonic TrustDbGrpcServer — full epistemic gRPC service (branch)
benchmarks.source: Branch session/conn-pillar. trustdb.proto: full gRPC service definition with epistemic semantics — View<T> three-state in responses, ConfidenceEnvelope on all data payloads, GovernanceArtifact audit trail per operation. TrustDbStore + TrustDbSync services. TrustDbGrpcServer: tonic implementation of store CRUD + telemetry. Feature-gated behind `server` (tonic + prost + tonic-build). build.rs for proto compilation. Pending: Arrow Flight adapter.

benchmarks.name: External Access
benchmarks.value: gRPC server (branch) + Swift C-FFI (merged) + Python PyO3 (branch)
benchmarks.source: Three access paths now exist: (1) gRPC TrustDbGrpcServer via tonic (branch session/conn-pillar), (2) Swift C-FFI for Apple in-process (merged PR #148), (3) Python PyO3 for data science in-process (branch). Arrow Flight SQL adapter pending.

sections.narrative: The Protocol/API story has advanced significantly. The semantic foundation (View<T> three-state, EBP state machine, SyncEnvelope/SyncDelta, GovernanceArtifact — 51 tests) is now complemented by two major additions: (1) Lens API (branch session/tep-g2-lens) — composable typed filters, composition algebra, compilation to TraversalPlan, serialization as GovernanceArtifact. (2) gRPC wire protocol (branch session/conn-pillar) — trustdb.proto with full epistemic semantics (View<T> in responses, ConfidenceEnvelope on payloads, GovernanceArtifact per operation), tonic TrustDbGrpcServer implementing store CRUD + telemetry. External access now has three paths: gRPC (remote), Swift C-FFI (Apple in-process), Python PyO3 (data science in-process). The Lens API is programmatic, not string-based — designed for AI agents rather than human SQL authors. Remaining gaps: Arrow Flight adapter for analytics tools (Polars, DuckDB, Tableau), streaming subscriptions, and the gap between 'implemented on branch' and 'merged + production-tested'.

history.reason: TEP semantic foundation implemented: View<T>, EBP, AssertionMeta, SyncEnvelope/SyncDelta, GovernanceArtifact. 5 files, 2,185 LOC, 51 tests passing. Lane A of TEP plan complete.

Lens API (WN-TEP-06-09) on branch tep-g2-lens. gRPC wire protocol (trustdb.proto + tonic server) on branch conn-pillar. External access now possible via gRPC (remote), Swift C-FFI (Apple), Python PyO3 (data science).
Arrow Flight SQL adapter (feature-gated). Entities/sections/edges as Arrow RecordBatch with confidence as native columns. PR #231 merged.

benchmarks.name: Built-in Telemetry
benchmarks.value: WAT counters (latency, throughput, cache hit rate)

benchmarks.name: Prometheus / OTel Integration
benchmarks.value: PrometheusExporter + OTel span constants — 11 new tests (branch)
benchmarks.source: Branch session/conn-pillar. PrometheusExporter: renders WAT counters as /metrics text exposition format. CustomGauge for DB-Gym results as Prometheus metrics. MetricLabels for multi-instance disambiguation. OTel span name constants and attribute keys (tracing integration). trustdb_span! macro for instrumented operations. 11 tests passing.

sections.narrative: Tooling has advanced from internal-only to ecosystem-integrated. WAT telemetry now exports to Prometheus via PrometheusExporter (text exposition format, CustomGauge for DB-Gym results, MetricLabels for multi-instance — 11 tests). OTel integration provides span name constants, attribute keys, and trustdb_span! macro for distributed tracing. DB-Gym provides 17 benchmark workloads. The TrustDB Scorecard is a live quality dashboard. Remaining gaps: Grafana dashboard templates (pairs with Prometheus), GUI admin panel (web-based entity/graph/confidence inspector), and CLI tool for interactive queries. The Prometheus + OTel work (branch) is the highest-leverage recent addition — it connects TrustDB to the standard observability stack that every DevOps team already runs.

tasks.task: Grafana dashboard templates (pre-built panels for key metrics)
